page: Microsoft Azure E20s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the ESv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 10 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 MB of L3 cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache per core. The instance is equipped with 160.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark evaluations position the E20s_v4 in the average performance tier across most compute and memory operations. The instance achieved a stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 1414.19 and a multi-core score of 18643.24. Memory bandwidth tests recorded 863.89 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, 114.17 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, and 61.14 GB/sec for large-block writes. In application-specific benchmarks, the server demonstrated a PassMark database operations score of 6693.11 and processed 17.08 million Redis SET operations per second. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression was a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% tier at 8.78 GB/sec, whereas multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reached an average tier of 74.86 GB/sec.

Given its high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation, the E20s_v4 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require local ephemeral storage. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs means users avoid paying for unused hardware assets, making it a specialized choice for external storage-dependent applications. It is highly suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, enterprise applications, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure E20s_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv4 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It provides 20 vCPUs (10 physical cores) and 160.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high 8.0 GB memory-to-vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks place the server in the average tier, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 18643.24 and a PassMark database operations score of 6693.11. It handles Redis SET operations at 17.08 million operations per second. While single-threaded bzip3 decompression is a relative bottleneck, its high memory capacity and dedicated compute resources make it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads. The E20s_v4 is well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise application hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 20 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 160 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability, delivering balanced mid-tier database and caching performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20s_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 20 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and Premium Storage support for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and a high memory-to-core ratio for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 20 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
160.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage or GPU resources
Delivers 17.08 million Redis SET operations per second in benchmark testing
Best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications
